t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Customer ID - platform-internal primary key. Assigned at registration. Unique within etoro DB. Used as the universal customer identifier across all tables.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Customer ID — platform-internal primary key. Assigned at registration. Sourced from Dim_Customer.RealCID.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Dropped 'Unique within etoro DB. Used as the universal customer identifier across all tables.'

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Group Customer ID - cross-product identity key linking the same person across eToro products/entities. NULL for older accounts predating GCID introduction.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Group Customer ID — cross-product identity key linking the same person across eToro products/entities. NULL for older accounts.

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Original customer ID from the source provider. Together with OriginalProviderID, enables tracing of migrated accounts. Default=0.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Original customer ID from the source provider. Together with OriginalProviderID, enables tracing of migrated accounts. Default=0.

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Customer login username. Unique (case-insensitive, enforced via UserName_LOWER computed column index).
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Customer login username. PII — dynamic data masking in UC.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Dropped uniqueness constraint, case-insensitivity, UserName_LOWER index enforcement; replaced with PII note not from upstream

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Affiliate (partner) ID under which the customer was acquired (renamed from SerialID). FK to BackOffice.Affiliate. NULL for direct/organic registrations.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Affiliate (partner) ID under which the customer was acquired. Sourced from Dim_Customer.AffiliateID (renamed).
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Dropped FK target (BackOffice.Affiliate) and NULL semantics for direct/organic registrations

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Customer email address. Unique (case-insensitive via LowerEmail computed column). Email changes trigger Customer.LastChanges update via trigger.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Customer email address. PII — masked with FUNCTION = 'default()'.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Dropped uniqueness, case-insensitivity, trigger semantics; replaced with PII note

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Gender: M, F, or U (Unknown). CHECK constraint CCST_GENDER enforces these three values only. Used in LinkedAccountHash1.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Gender: M, F, or U (Unknown). PII.

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Country of residence. FK to Dictionary.Country. Determines regulatory framework, available instruments, and leverage limits. Default=0.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Country of residence. FK to Dictionary.Country. Determines regulatory framework, available instruments, and leverage limits.

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Customer date of birth. Used in LinkedAccountHash1 for duplicate detection and in KYC age verification.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Customer date of birth. PII.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Dropped LinkedAccountHash1 duplicate detection and KYC age verification usage

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Referral CID - the customer who referred this customer (for RAF program tracking).
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Referral CID — the customer who referred this customer (for RAF program tracking).

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Platform download source ID. Legacy tracking for which platform installer the customer used.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Platform download source ID. Legacy acquisition tracking.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Slight rewording: 'which platform installer the customer used' → 'acquisition tracking'

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Advertising banner ID that led to registration. Legacy acquisition tracking.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Advertising banner ID that led to registration. Legacy acquisition tracking.

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Sub-affiliate identifier string. Can be up to 1024 chars for complex affiliate tracking paths.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Sub-affiliate identifier string. Sourced from Dim_Customer.SubSerialID (renamed).
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Dropped 'Can be up to 1024 chars for complex affiliate tracking paths'

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Version of the privacy policy the customer has accepted. FK to Dictionary.PrivacyPolicy.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Version of the privacy policy the customer has accepted.

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Registration IP address.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Registration IP address (stored as bigint). PII — masked.

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Regulatory entity governing this account. FK to Dictionary.Regulation. Top values: CySEC=7.39M, BVI=7.30M, FCA=1.17M. Changes trigger RegulationChangeDate update.

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Electronic verification match result. Score or decision from automated identity verification vendors (Onfido, Au10tix). NULL if not yet processed.

t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Secondary/override regulation for accounts subject to multiple jurisdictions. Synced from Dim_Customer.DesignatedRegulationID with change detection.

issues.column_or_section: Country, Region, Language, Club, Channel, SubChannel, LabelName, PotentialDesk, FunnelName, FunnelFromName, CommunicationLanguage, Verified, Manager, State, NewMarketingRegion
issues.problem: All 16 dimension lookup columns tagged Tier 2 (SP_CIDFirstDates, Dim_X) but they are dim-lookup passthroughs (SELECT dim.Name with no transform). Per tier rules, these should be Tier 1 with the dim's root origin (e.g., Dictionary.Country for Country/Region, Dictionary.Language for Language, Dictionary.PlayerLevel for Club, etc.).

Tagged Tier 1 but no upstream wiki exists for Function_Population_Funded or Function_Population_First_Time_Funded. These are ETL-computed within SP_CIDFirstDates via inline function calls. Should be Tier 2 — SP_CIDFirstDates.
Tier 1 column paraphrased: dropped vendor names (Onfido, Au10tix) and NULL semantics ('NULL if not yet processed'). Replaced upstream content with SP implementation detail ('change detection').

Tagged Tier 2 (SP_Dim_Customer ← CustomerFinanceDB) but these are direct reads from Dim_Customer (alias dc, confirmed in lineage). Dim_Customer wiki documents both columns. Should be Tier 1 quoting Dim_Customer wiki verbatim.
No row count or date range. Section 1 says 'for every valid customer' but never quantifies. P3 (live data sampling) was skipped. Self-assessed quality of 9.5/10 is not supported by evidence.

verdict.regeneration_feedback: Re-run with: (1) Re-tier all 16 dim-lookup columns as Tier 1 using each dim's root production origin (e.g., Country → Tier 1 — Dictionary.Country, Region → Tier 1 — Dictionary.Country, Language → Tier 1 — Dictionary.Language, Club → Tier 1 — Dictionary.PlayerLevel). Quote the dim wiki description verbatim. (2) Re-tier IsFundedNew, FirstNewFundedDate, LastNewFundedDate as Tier 2 — SP_CIDFirstDates, Function_Population_Funded. (3) Re-tier FirstDepositDate and FirstDepositAmount as Tier 1, quoting Dim_Customer wiki verbatim. (4) Fix all 18 Tier 1 descriptions to verbatim-quote the upstream Dim_Customer wiki — specifically restore: EvMatchStatus vendor names (Onfido, Au10tix) and NULL semantics; UserName uniqueness constraint; Email uniqueness and trigger; Gender CHECK constraint; SerialID FK and NULL for organic; RegulationID specific counts. (5) Run P3 live data sampling to add row count and date range to Section 1. (6) Correct self-assessed quality score from 9.5 to reflect actual tier and fidelity state.
